byALEXX ALTMAN-DEVILBISS | The National Desk

LOS ANGELES (TND)  —A man pretending to have a gun hijacked a bus in Los Angeles and grabbed the wheel causing the bus to hit several vehicles and crash into a hotel, according to police.

TheLos Angeles Police Department(LAPD) said there were no passengers on the bus when the man boarded around 11:10 p.m. Wednesday.

Police said the man approached the Metro bus operator, made verbal threats and reached into his waistband as if he had a gun and said "Just Drive!"

Hijacked bus in Los Angeles collides with cars and crashes into hotel (LAPD)

"The bus operator attempted to make a left turn from Olympic Boulevard onto Georgia Street when the suspect grabbed the steering wheel and caused the bus to collide with several vehicles. One of the vehicles on Georgia Street was occupied," according to police.

Police said the bus crashed into a hotel shortly after and the man fled the scene on foot.

Officers found a handgun on the sidewalk in front of the bus that was later determined to be a BB gun.

The Metro bus driver and the person in the vehicle the bus collided with were taken to a nearby hospital by Los Angeles Fire Department paramedics.

Police said the bus driver has since been released and the person in the vehicle is listed in stable condition.

The suspect was identified as Anai Lindsey of Nevada and he was charged with kidnapping. He is being held on a $100,000 bond.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Hijacked bus in Los Angeles collides with cars and crashes into hotel
description: A man pretending to have a gun hijacked a bus in Los Angeles and grabbed the wheel causing the bus to hit several vehicles and crash into a hotel, police said.
